name: Vivek Ash
problem: This maize (corn) ear is clearly showing a severe ear rot disease, most likely Fusarium ear rot or Aspergillus ear rot (both are common fungal infections in corn).
advice:
Solution for Maize Ear Rot (Fusarium / Aspergillus):

Remove and destroy infected ears immediately to stop spread.
Avoid excess moisture – ensure proper drainage and do not over-irrigate.
Harvest crops on time; delayed harvesting increases infection risk.
Dry grains properly after harvest (moisture below 13–14%).
Use resistant or disease-tolerant maize varieties.
Practice crop rotation (avoid growing maize repeatedly in the same field).
Control insects (like corn borers), as they create entry points for fungi.
Apply recommended fungicides (e.g., Carbendazim or Mancozeb) if infection is severe.

name: Himanshu Kumar
problem: My rice plan is getting day by day black hoe can i stop it by this
advice:
Rice plants turning black – causes & solution

Rice plants turning black can be due to fungal disease, bacterial infection, or pest attack.

1. Fungal disease (Blast / Brown spot)
Leaves get black or brown spots and slowly dry.
Solution: Spray Tricyclazole (0.6 g per liter water) or Carbendazim. Repeat after 7–10 days.

2. Bacterial leaf blight
Leaf tips turn yellow, then brown, then black.
Solution: Spray Streptocycline (0.5 g) + Copper Oxychloride (2 g) per liter water.

3. Pest attack (Brown planthopper)
Plants turn black in patches and dry. Insects are present at the base.
Solution: Spray Imidacloprid or Thiamethoxam.

Important precautions:

Avoid excess urea fertilizer
Maintain proper water drainage
Check plants regularly

name: Rahul Chaudhary
problem: My wheat leaf is getting yellow leaf
advice: Probable Nitrogen Deficiency or Iron Chlorosis: If uniform yellowing, apply Urea (20kg/hectare). If interveinal yellowing (veins green), apply Iron sulphate (5kg/hectare).

name: Vivek Ash
problem: This is a maize (corn) ear heavily infested by insects, most likely the corn earworm (Helicoverpa armigera) along with secondary pests and fungal infection.
advice: Use neem oil or insecticidal soap and inspect plants daily for early signs of infestation.
